title: Commelina hookeri
taxonomy: Commelina hookeri is classified within the family Commelinaceae. This family is part of the order Commelinales. The order Commelinales is further classified under the class Liliopsida, also known as monocots. The class Liliopsida is a subgroup of the larger phylum Angiospermophyta, which includes flowering plants. The phylum Angiospermophyta is a division of the larger kingdom Plantae.
uses: Commelina hookeri is known for its various medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Indian medicine. The plant is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. It is also believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin infections. Furthermore, Commelina hookeri is used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's leaves and roots are used in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and ointments, to harness its medicinal benefits.

intro: Commelina hookeri, commonly known as Hooker's dayflower or small blue dayflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Commelinaceae. Traditionally, Commelina hookeri has been used in Ayurvedic and folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Commelina hookeri is commonly used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, and is also used as a topical treatment for skin conditions like eczema and wounds.
